title: The Dark Tower Re-Read / Read - Dungeon Dive Book Club
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ktZgSqvfl8o

A three-minute video that launches a nine-month expedition. Daniel (@dungeondive) announces the Book Club's most ambitious undertaking: reading the entire Dark Tower series, starting with the revised Gunslinger in October and running seven to nine months. This is no casual read-along. Daniel is bringing Bev Vincent's The Road to the Dark Tower as a companion study, plus a stack of Field Notes notebooks — one per volume — for serious annotation. It's at least his fifth full journey to the Tower (The Gunslinger alone he's read six or seven times), and he declares it his favourite book series of all time, full stop. The club structure gets an upgrade too: a Facebook group (about ten members signed up) for chapter-by-chapter discussion, with a Google Meet at the end of each month. The group spans first-time readers to veterans, prompting a house rule on spoiler courtesy — Daniel himself is immune to spoilers, but promises to protect those who aren't. Open questions remain charmingly unresolved: will the read include The Wind Through the Keyhole, and does Insomnia count as an honorary numbered entry? For anyone who later followed the channel's Ran(King) of King series, this is where that road began.

----

Daniel packed a companion volume and per-book notebooks for his fifth trip to the Tower. Have you ever studied a beloved series that seriously on a reread — and what did the apparatus reveal?
